title: Metabolomics and ultra-processed foods intake: A scoping review protocol
author: Peixoto, Rocha, et al.

steps.title: Description of protocol
steps.instruction: This review will use the JBI [23] methodology and the PRISMA-ScR extension [24]. The protocol was registered on the OSF [25] under the DOI 10.17605/OSF.IO/3YDWA. To check for previous reviews, a preliminary search was conducted on April 1, 2026, across JBI Evidence Synthesis, the Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews, PubMed, and PROSPERO. No reviews or protocols focused on metabolomics studies and UPFs consumption were found, reinforcing this proposal's relevance.

steps.title: Description of protocol
steps.instruction: This study seeks to answer the following main question: What is the current state of knowledge regarding studies on metabolomics and UPFs consumption? Additionally, the following secondary questions will be addressed: (a) which analytical platforms and metabolomic approaches (targeted vs. untargeted) have been most commonly used; (b) which biological matrices have been analyzed; (c) which metabolite profiles or metabolomic signatures have been associated with UPFs consumption; and (d) in which countries and population contexts have the studies been conducted?

steps.title: Data extraction
steps.instruction: Data extraction will be performed independently by two reviewers using a form previously developed by the authors (Table 3). The instrument will be tested in advance for suitability and adjusted if needed during the review. Reviewer discrepancies will be resolved by consensus or, if necessary, by consulting a third reviewer. If information is incomplete or missing, study authors may be contacted for clarification.Table 3Data extraction.Table 3 dummy alt textCategoryDataBibliographic informationAuthor(s)TitleYear of publicationCountry where the study was conductedJournal or publication sourceDigital Object Identifier (DOI)Study characteristicsObjective(s)Study DesignContext/PopulationSample SizeParticipant InformationGenderAgeHealth statusAssessment of UPFs consumptionMethod of dietary measurement and assessmentMetabolomic analysisBiological matrixAnalytical platformApproach (targeted or untargeted)Statistical methodsKey ResultsSummary of key findingsSignificant associationsKey FindingsDiscussion of key findingsReported limitations and strengthsObservationsAny additional information specific to the research question or objectivesUPFs, ultra-processed foods.

steps.title: Analysis of the evidence
steps.instruction: The evidence will be presented descriptively, in accordance with the JBI recommendations for scoping reviews. The data will be organized and presented using simple descriptive statistics, including absolute and relative frequencies of the main characteristics of the studies, such as study design, population, methods for assessing food intake, and metabolomic approaches used.

steps.title: Analysis of the evidence
steps.instruction: In addition, a descriptive synthesis of the findings will be conducted to identify the main approaches, knowledge gaps, and patterns found in the literature. No quantitative synthesis of the results or assessment of methodological quality for the purpose of drawing inferences will be performed, given the exploratory nature of this scoping review.
